heading: Authority to suspend inspection
label: 3101 Authority to suspend inspection
text: When the President decides that the needs of foreign commerce require, the President may suspend a provision of this part for a foreign-built vessel registered as a vessel of the United States on conditions the President may specify.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource section (U.S. Code)310146:82Section 3101 provides the President with the authority to suspend the inspection laws for a foreign-built reflagged U.S. vessel when the needs of foreign commerce require this. For example, if a foreign-flag vessel is reflagged in the U.S. but does not meet the specific Coast Guard inspection requirements, the President could suspend these requirements.

Effective Date, , , provided that: [Part B of subtitle II and sections 7306 (related to able seaman sail) and 7311 of title 46 effective , in absence of regulations for sailing school vessels under part B on that date.]Pub. L. 98–89, § 2(g)(1)97 Stat. 599Aug. 26, 1983Apr. 15, 1984“Part B [chapters 31 to 43] of subtitle II and sections 7306 (related to able seaman sail) and 7311 of title 46 (as enacted by section 1 of this Act) take effect , or when regulations for sailing school vessels under part B are effective, whichever is earlier.”April 15, 1984
